dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5815 IH: District of Columbia Medicaid Fairness Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Cloud (for himself, Mr. Arrington, Ms. Van Duyne, Mr. Tiffany, Mr. Clyde, Mr. Brecheen, Mr. Steube, Mr. Roy, and Mr. Harris of Maryland) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title XIX of the Social Security Act to bring fairness to the Medicaid Federal medical assistance percentage for the District of Columbia.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the District of Columbia Medicaid Fairness Act.

section.header: Adjustment of FMAP for District of Columbia
section.text: Section 1905 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the first sentence of subsection (b), by striking this title and everything that follows through shall be 70 percent and inserting title XXI, the Federal medical assistance percentage for the District of Columbia shall be 70 percent, and for purposes of this title, for fiscal years before 2030, the Federal medical assistance percentage for the District of Columbia shall be equal to the percentage that would otherwise be determined under this subsection without application of this clause or the percentage specified in subsection (ll) for such fiscal year (whichever is greater), and for fiscal year 2030 and each subsequent fiscal year, the Federal medical assistance percentage for the District of Columbia shall be equal to the percentage that would otherwise be determined under this subsection without application of this clause; and
by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Federal medical assistance percentage for the District of Columbia
subsection.text: For purposes of clause (3) of the first sentence of subsection (b), the percentage specified in this subsection for a fiscal year is—

paragraph.text: for a fiscal year before fiscal year 2027, 70 percent;
